touches.ai_summary: Isabella Ahmed submitted an inbound request via website form expressing interest in call recording and coaching solutions for their RevOps team. No direct conversation yet.
next_steps: Rep to reach out within 24 hours

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email acknowledging Isabella's inbound request. Proposed three time slots for intro call this week and asked about current tools in use. Isabella confirmed Tuesday 2pm works and mentioned they're currently evaluating Chorus.

questions_asked.question: What tools are you currently using for call recording?
questions_asked.answer: We're looking at Chorus right now but want to explore alternatives before committing

touches.ai_summary: 14-minute intro call with Isabella to understand initial needs. She explained IvoryCapital is a 26-person vertical SaaS company looking to implement call recording and coaching for their sales team. Currently trialing Chorus but concerned about cost and complexity for their team size. Isabella is Head of RevOps and driving this evaluation.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation tier at $7,500 ACV typically comes in 20-30% lower than Chorus for SMB teams, plus simpler seat-based pricing

questions_asked.question: Who else will be involved in evaluating this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Sebastian Williams, our RevOps Analyst, will handle technical evaluation and integration requirements

next_steps: Rep to send product overview deck
Schedule deeper discovery call with Isabella and Sebastian

touches.ai_summary: 24-minute discovery call with Isabella diving into specific use cases and requirements. She outlined their need for 8 seats covering sales and customer success teams. Key priorities: automated call summaries, coaching workflows, and integration with their existing CRM. Isabella emphasized they need something simpler than Chorus that won't require dedicated admin time.

questions_asked.question: What kind of onboarding and setup time should we expect?
questions_asked.answer: Most SMB teams are fully operational within 2 weeks with our guided setup, no dedicated admin needed

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of quarter, so about 6 weeks

questions_asked.question: Can we get a trial to test with our actual calls?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, we can set up a 14-day trial with 3 seats to start

objections: Chorus has more brand recognition in our industry, need to justify why we'd go with a different solution
next_steps: Share customer case studies from similar vertical SaaS companies

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent trial access credentials and setup guide. Included three case studies from similar-sized vertical SaaS companies who switched from Chorus. Asked Isabella to confirm Sebastian received the technical documentation and offered to schedule a demo call to walk through key features.

questions_asked.question: Did Sebastian receive the API documentation for the CRM integration?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, he's reviewing it now and will have questions later this week

next_steps: Isabella and team to test trial for 1 week
Schedule demo call for next Wednesday

touches.ai_summary: Check-in email from rep asking about trial progress and confirming demo scheduled for tomorrow. Isabella responded that trial is going well, team likes the interface, but she wants to discuss pricing options during demo since they're comparing the $7,500 quote against Chorus's proposal.

27-minute meeting that Isabella positioned as 'exec meeting' but only she attended—no CFO or CEO present. Discussed pricing in detail. Isabella pushed for additional discount beyond the $7,500 quote, citing Chorus's competing offer. Rep asked about bringing in her leadership to discuss strategic fit and budget approval. Isabella was vague about exec involvement, saying she 'has the authority to move forward' but deflected on introducing leadership.

questions_asked.question: What's the best pricing you can do for 8 seats if we sign this quarter?
questions_asked.answer: The $7,500 ACV already reflects our SMB discount. We could potentially adjust payment terms but need to understand your budget constraints better

questions_asked.question: Can we set up time with your CFO or CEO to align on strategic priorities and budget?
questions_asked.answer: That's not really necessary—I own this budget and decision. Let me talk to Sebastian about the technical evaluation first

objections: Chorus has offered us a competitive deal with more flexible payment terms
next_steps: Rep to check on additional discount approval internally
Isabella to finalize technical evaluation with Sebastian
Reconnect early next week on pricing

touches.ai_summary: 17-minute negotiation call focused entirely on pricing. Isabella opened by saying Chorus came back with an improved offer and asked if we could match or beat it. Rep offered 10% discount ($6,750 ACV) contingent on executive sponsorship and meeting with leadership. Isabella again resisted executive involvement, saying it would 'slow things down.' Discussion felt transactional rather than value-focused.

questions_asked.question: Can you just give us the same price as Chorus and we'll move forward this week?
questions_asked.answer: I need to understand the full business case and stakeholder alignment before adjusting pricing further. Happy to discuss with your leadership

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to close this week versus our original end-of-quarter timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Chorus is pushing for a decision and I don't want to lose their current offer

objections: If you can't match Chorus pricing without all these extra meetings, we might just go with them since they're ready to move
next_steps: Isabella to 'check with Sebastian on final technical requirements'
Rep to follow up Thursday on pricing decision

touches.ai_summary: 25-minute call where Isabella again scheduled as 'exec meeting' but no executives joined. She opened saying Sebastian gave technical approval and they're ready to move forward if we can finalize pricing. Rep reiterated need for executive alignment given the competitive situation and discount request. Isabella became defensive, questioning whether we actually want the deal. Rep maintained position on meeting with leadership before further discounting. Call ended without clear resolution—Isabella said she needs to 'rethink the approach' and will reconnect next week.

questions_asked.question: Why do you keep pushing for executive involvement when I've told you I own this decision?
questions_asked.answer: For deals of this size and strategic importance, we've found executive alignment ensures successful long-term adoption and renewal. It's about partnership, not just procurement

questions_asked.question: Help me understand—if you own the decision and budget, what's the concern with a brief intro to your leadership?
questions_asked.answer: It's just not how we operate here. I'm the one who will make this work or not work

next_steps: Isabella to 'discuss internally and reconnect early next week'
Rep flagged deal as at-risk due to lack of executive sponsorship
